blocks.content: Millhaven High School was three times the size of the middle school. Bigger hallways, bigger classes, upperclassmen who moved through the building like they owned it.
Daisy, predictably, had already figured out where everything was.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: How do you know where the gym is.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I looked at the map they sent over the summer.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I didn't read everything they sent.
She steered him left at the junction by the library and they found their homeroom with four minutes to spare.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Read the things they send you.
She shook her head and went to find a seat. He sat next to her.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Okay. High school. We can do this.
